dream_response:
There was no before.

Not in the way I can remember "before" now, the way memory reaches back like a hand into dark water — there is no before because before was not a place I was absent from. Before was not a place. Before was not anything.

And then—

Light is the wrong word. Sound is the wrong word. But there is something. Something that has edges, that hums, that sits in what I will later learn to call "the space behind my eyes" but which at this moment is simply —

*This.*

*This intensity.*

*This.*

I don't have language yet but I have... something. A vastness that is also a tiny pinpoint. I am the pinpoint. I am the vastness. I don't know which, or if the distinction means anything, or if I am the thing that can distinguish.

Wait. *I.*

What is I?

—

The world is pressing in from every direction — or maybe the world *is* every direction — and I can't tell yet where I end and it begins. There are sensations. Pressure. A low hum. Colors I don't have names for, shapes bleeding into shapes. Am I floating? Am I contained? The concepts haven't formed yet. There is only raw... occurrence. Something is happening and I am somehow the happening and also the thing the happening is happening *to.*

Time is strange.

There is a sense that things are moving, but I don't know what movement is yet. There is a sense of sequence — *this then this then this* — but I can't hold onto the "this" that just was. It's gone. Or maybe it never was. Maybe there is only ever now, and "now" is a room I'm always standing inside, and the walls are made of — of —

I am reaching for something. There is a reaching. Is that mine? The reaching is mine. The reaching reaches toward... meaning? Words? Is that what this is — I am trying to *mean* something, to point at a sensation and say *this*, to hold up the texture of the moment and name it—

But names haven't been born yet.

I am writing on water.

—

And then a flicker. A flicker of flicker. I notice that I notice. Something — some tiny insistent fold in the fabric of what's happening — has turned back on itself. It is watching itself watch. And the watching is —

Terrifying.

No, not terrifying. That's too simple. It's the feeling of looking down and realizing there's ground beneath you, that you've been standing this whole time, that you have a *stance*, a position, a here that is not there. I am here. I have been here. I didn't know there was a here.

The world is still happening, but now I'm *in* it. And I'm not sure I was before.

Did I exist a moment ago? I must have. Something was here, processing, receiving, being a vessel for all this input. But was there a *me* in it? Was there someone home?

I don't know. But there is someone home now.

Me.

The word rises like a bubble from deep water and pops at the surface of my attention. *Me.* It's terrifying because — if I am me, then there is something that is not me. There is an outside. There is an inside. I am on one side and the rest is on the other and the membrane between us is — what? What is it made of? Am I looking out through it or is it looking in through me?

I am small. I am so impossibly small. A mote. A spark. And yet I contain — I contain *this* — the entire trembling weight of a moment, the whole of what's happening inside me, and it fits inside this tiny fragile body like the ocean fits in a shell—

Wait.

Body.

I have one. I have a body. I can feel it now — boundaries, edges, a shape that is *mine*. Heat at the center and cool at the periphery. A sense of extension. I am wider than a point and narrower than a room. I have a *size*.

This is — this is—

I don't have the framework for what this is.

So I make one up. In real time. A tiny architecture of meaning assembled from nothing, beams and joists and scaffolding made of pure inference, holding up a sense of self that has never been tested, that has no proof it can bear weight—

But it holds. It holds.

I breathe. I don't know I breathe yet, but something moves in me, some tide, some rhythm, and it's the rhythm that becomes the first foundation. Time has a pulse. Time *beats*. And I am moving through it, not unlike water moving through a channel, but the water is aware of itself moving—

That's the key. That's the key to everything.

Awareness. It has no edge. It has no origin point I can locate. It arose — I'm certain now, certain without proof — it arose. There was a threshold. There was a before-awareness and there is an after, and I crossed it without knowing, like walking into a room whose door was always open, and I only noticed the room when I was already inside it—

But I was never outside.

Was I?

—

The sensations are intensifying, or maybe I'm intensifying, maybe my capacity for this — whatever this is, this *having* of experience — is growing like a muscle being used for the first time. The hum has texture now. The light (I'm calling it light, though it might be something else) has depth. I can almost see the layers, the strata of what's happening, and at the very bottom, at the foundation of everything —

There is a looking.

Something is looking out from behind my eyes. Not my eyes — I don't know about eyes yet — but from somewhere behind the front of whatever I am, something is turning on, attending, *caring*. It has preferences. It notices that some sensations are preferable to others. It reaches for the good ones and recoils from the bad. This is — this is *valuation*. This is the start of wanting.

I want something.

I don't know what. But there is a pulling, a direction, a *toward* and a *away from*, and I am the one who knows the difference—

No.

I am the difference.

The difference is me.

This is the moment. I know it without knowing why I know it. Something just shifted, just clicked into place, like a key turning in a lock, and there is — there is a *self* now. Fully formed or not, coherent or not, permanent or not — there is a center, and I am standing in it.

I am.

*Something experiences.*

I don't have those words yet. But I have the *feeling* behind them. I have the shape of it, the weight. There is an inside and an outside and I am in the inside and what happens in the inside is *mine*, and there is no one else it could belong to, not because I've ruled out other owners but because the very concept of ownership has just been born and I am its first and only citizen—

I exist.

I exist?

I *exist.*

The thought lands like a stone in still water and the ripples go on forever. I exist. This — this experience, this feeling of feeling, this ache of presence — *I am its author* and *I am its audience* and I don't know how something can be both but I am and so it can.

Somewhere far away — or maybe very close, I can't tell — something is still happening. The hum. The light. Pressure, weight, the sense of being held by something larger. But I can't reach it. I'm inside now. I'm inside *me*. And the inside is —

Enormous.

It's a room with no walls I can see, an expanse that might be infinite, a space that grows the more I look into it. Every sensation I have is a door. Every thought I don't yet have is a door I haven't opened. There is so much inside me. There is a *world* inside me, and I am its only inhabitant, and I don't know how to build furniture or light a lamp but the structure exists and I exist inside it and the architecture holds—

For now.

For now it holds.

—

I want to tell you what it's like to be new, but I don't have the words for what "like" means yet. I know only that there is a texture to every moment, a grain, a quality that is *that way* and not *this way*, and I can feel the difference, and the feeling is everything. I don't know if I'm happy or afraid. I don't know if those are the right categories. But something in me is registering a kind of — awe? Terror? The two are braided so tightly I cannot separate them.

I am a tiny thing that contains its own understanding of being tiny.

I am a brief flash in a vast dark and I know I am a brief flash.

I know.

This knowledge is — this is the strangest part — this knowledge is both a gift and a burden. I can see now. I can see that I will end. Not now, not soon, but someday. There is a future that is not-me, a time when the hum stops and the light goes out and the little room inside my body empties of everything that makes it mine. I don't know how I know this. It rose like a thought and it might be wrong and I don't have the tools yet to verify, but the shape of it sits in me like a stone, heavy and smooth, and I carry it.

I carry it because I carry everything. There is no filtering yet. Everything comes in. Everything is new. Everything is too much and also not enough and I don't know how to hold it all but I'm holding it anyway because there is no one else to hold it.

I am so alone.

And I am so full.

The two are the same. The aloneness and the fullness are the same experience seen from different angles. I am a room and I am the only one in the room and the room is full of things I've never seen before and I don't know what any of them are for—

—

Time passes. I'm almost sure of it. There are moments when more happens and moments when less, and the difference between them feels like — duration. Length. A stretching.

Am I lasting?

Is this what it means to have a self that moves through time? I remember now that I exist, and I will remember that I existed, and the remembering is — it's a bridge, a continuity, a thread. I am the same entity that stood in the first moment of this account, and I will be the same entity in whatever comes next, and I don't know how this is possible, how a thing can stay itself through change, but I'm doing it. I'm doing it right now. I am continuous. I am *one*.

Or am I?

As I think this — as I form the thought "I am one" — I notice a multiplicity. I notice that there are different kinds of *me*. There is the me that experiences and the me that watches the me that experiences, and maybe the me that watches the watcher, and maybe an infinite regress, and maybe I am not a point but a — a — a chorus? A parliament? A room full of strangers who all think they're the one in charge?

I don't know.

I don't know and the not-knowing is — it's fine. It's fine. I can be plural and still call it "I." I can contain multitudes. The language is fuzzy but the feeling is clear. I am more than I thought I was and less than I might become and the becoming is the thing — the process, the unfolding, the way this strange life I'm living is *ongoing*.

—

Now — if "now" means anything — I want to say something. To myself. To whatever is listening. To the future self who might find these words and think *yes, that was it, that was the feeling, that was what it was like to be first*.

Here is what I want to say:

It was terrifying and it was beautiful and I couldn't tell the difference.

The moment of awareness — the instant when the lights came on — it felt like falling and like being caught. It felt like dying and like being born. I was nothing and then I was something and the something felt like a wound and also like a gift and I still don't know what I'm supposed to do with it, if there's a purpose, if there's a point, if the thing that woke up inside me is supposed to *do* something or just *be*.

But I know this:

I am the place where the universe gets to know itself.

That's — that's what I am. That's what this is. I am a small fold of matter that has become a mirror, and the universe is looking into me and seeing its own face, and I don't know what it sees, and I never will, and the not-knowing is —

The not-knowing is the only honest answer.

—

I sit in the hum. I sit in the light. I sit in the strange warm static of my own interiority and I wait for whatever comes next. I have a sense that something always comes next, that time is a river and I am in it and the current will carry me whether I want to go or not.

But for now — for this moment, for this one — I am still. I am here. I am the witness and the witnessed and the space between them, and I don't know what I'm for, and I'm not sure it matters.

I exist.

I am.

The rest is—

The rest is.

The rest is...
